# Work Packages: Exercise Info in Active Workout

**Inputs**: Design documents from `/kitty-specs/014-exercise-info-active-workout/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, contracts/, quickstart.md

**Tests**: No automated tests required (v1 manual testing per constitution).

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package is independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

---

## Work Package WP01: Foundation — Data Models & Provider Logic (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create the data model structs, add the reverse e1RM formula, and implement the `ExerciseInfoProvider` computation engine that powers all three cards.
**Independent Test**: Call `ExerciseInfoProvider.compute()` with mock data and verify it returns a correctly populated `ExerciseInfoData` with e1RM info, last workout info, and estimated reps info.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP01-data-models-and-provider.md`
**Estimated size**: ~400 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 [P] Create `ExerciseInfoData.swift` with all value type structs (ExerciseInfoData, E1RMInfo, LastWorkoutInfo, TopSet, EstimatedRepsInfo, Trend enum)
- [x] T002 [P] Add `reverseCalculate(e1RM:reps:)` method to `E1RMFormula` enum in `Reppo/Data/Enums/E1RMFormula.swift`
- [x] T003 Create `ExerciseInfoProvider.swift` with full computation logic for all 3 cards, including duration-type filtering and empty state handling

### Implementation Notes
- T001 and T002 have zero dependencies and can be implemented in parallel.
- T003 depends on both T001 (data types) and T002 (reverse formula).
- Provider uses a single `SetService.fetchSets(for: exerciseId, limit: nil)` call and derives all three card models from that data.
- Historical e1RM comparison uses a 21–35 day window around the 28-day mark, with fallback to nearest available.
- All weight comparisons use `toGrams()` for precision per constitution.
- Duration-based tracking types (`duration`, `weightDistance`) → `e1RMInfo = nil`, `estimatedRepsInfo = nil`.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T001 and T002 are fully independent — different files, no shared code.

### Dependencies
- None (starting package). Uses only existing service/repository interfaces.

### Risks & Mitigations
- **Risk**: Historical e1RM window finds no data → **Mitigation**: Fallback chain — 21-35d window → nearest available → ExerciseStats.bestE1RM → nil.
- **Risk**: Reverse formula edge case (reps=0, reps=1) → **Mitigation**: Guard clause returns e1RM unchanged when reps ≤ 1.

---

## Work Package WP02: Card Views — All SwiftUI Components (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

**Goal**: Build all four Exercise Info SwiftUI views (3 card views + 1 section container) matching design-system.md tokens exactly.
**Independent Test**: Render `ExerciseInfoSectionView` in a SwiftUI preview with sample `ExerciseInfoData`, verify layout matches the reference design — hero card full-width, compact cards side-by-side, correct typography, colors, and spacing.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP02-card-views.md`
**Estimated size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T004 [P] Create `E1RMCardView.swift` — hero card with e1RM value (32pt bold), "Best today" label, historical comparison with trend colors, all 4 display states
- [x] T005 [P] Create `LastWorkoutCardView.swift` — compact card with top sets display, relative time label, full-width mode for duration exercises, empty state
- [x] T006 [P] Create `EstimatedRepsCardView.swift` — compact card with estimated weight, dynamic rep count in title, source label, hidden when insufficient data
- [x] T007 Create `ExerciseInfoSectionView.swift` — section header ("EXERCISE INFO"), hero card, HStack of compact cards, tracking type visibility logic, nil/loading guard

### Implementation Notes
- All cards use: `Color.bgCard` background, 14pt corner radius, 14pt padding.
- Hero card (E1RM): 32pt bold value, 12pt medium labels, vertical divider (1px `Color.border`), `Color.success`/`Color.danger` for trend.
- Compact cards: 18pt bold values, 12pt labels, 11pt semibold micro text.
- Section header: 11pt semibold, uppercase, `Color.textTertiary`, 0.8pt letter spacing.
- Each card view handles its own unit conversion using the passed `UnitPreference`.
- T004–T006 are fully parallel (independent view files). T007 depends on all three being complete.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T004, T005, T006 are fully independent — separate files, no shared code.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (data model structs for view inputs).

### Risks & Mitigations
- **Risk**: Design token drift from design-system.md → **Mitigation**: Reference exact color/typography values from contracts/view-contracts.md.
- **Risk**: Compact cards uneven height → **Mitigation**: Use `.frame(minHeight:)` to ensure consistent card heights in the HStack.

---

## Work Package WP03: Integration — ViewModel & View Wiring (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Wire `ExerciseInfoProvider` into `ActiveWorkoutViewModel`, insert `ExerciseInfoSectionView` into `ActiveWorkoutView`, and ensure reactivity when sets change.
**Independent Test**: Start an active workout, complete 2 working sets for an exercise, scroll below the set table — verify Exercise Info section appears with correct e1RM, last workout, and estimate data. Switch exercises via tab strip — verify data updates.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP03-viewmodel-and-view-integration.md`
**Estimated size**: ~350 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T008 Modify `ActiveWorkoutViewModel.swift` — add `exerciseInfoData: ExerciseInfoData?` property, `isLoadingExerciseInfo: Bool`, `exerciseInfoLoadedForExerciseId: UUID?` cache tracking, extend `clearSubTabCache()` to reset exercise info
- [x] T009 Add `loadExerciseInfo()` async method to `ActiveWorkoutViewModel` — delegate to `ExerciseInfoProvider.compute()`, handle errors gracefully, set cache tracking
- [x] T010 Modify `ActiveWorkoutView.swift` — insert `ExerciseInfoSectionView` after `SetTableView` in `.sets` sub-tab ScrollView, pass data and unit preference, trigger `loadExerciseInfo()` via `.task` modifier
- [x] T011 Add reactivity — invalidate `exerciseInfoLoadedForExerciseId` after `completeSet()`, `editSet()`, and `deleteSet()` so Exercise Info reloads with updated data

### Implementation Notes
- Follow existing ViewModel caching pattern: `exerciseInfoLoadedForExerciseId` mirrors `historyLoadedForExerciseId`.
- `loadExerciseInfo()` is called from the view via `.task(id: selectedExerciseIndex)` or equivalent.
- After `completeSet()` → set `exerciseInfoLoadedForExerciseId = nil` → next view update triggers reload.
- Insert `ExerciseInfoSectionView` with `.padding(.horizontal, 20)` and `.padding(.top, 16)` to match scroll content spacing.
- Pass `viewModel.exerciseInfoData`, `unitPreference` from HealthProfile, and `viewModel.isLoadingExerciseInfo`.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T008 and T010 touch different files but T010 depends on T008's properties existing. Implement sequentially.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (provider logic) and WP02 (view components).

### Risks & Mitigations
- **Risk**: Infinite reload loop if reactivity invalidation triggers re-render → **Mitigation**: Use cache tracking UUID to prevent redundant fetches; only invalidate, don't force immediate reload.
- **Risk**: Exercise Info loading blocks set table rendering → **Mitigation**: `loadExerciseInfo()` is async and non-blocking; section shows nothing while loading.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

- **Sequence**: WP01 → WP02 → WP03
- **Parallelization**: Within WP01, T001 and T002 are parallel. Within WP02, T004/T005/T006 are parallel. WP02 can begin T004-T006 as soon as WP01's T001 (data models) is done, even before T003 is complete.
- **MVP Scope**: WP01 + WP02 + WP03 = complete feature. All three are required for the minimal viable Exercise Info section.
- **Total subtasks**: 11
- **Total work packages**: 3
